items.blurb: Wembanyama’s 39 points lead Spurs past Timberwolves in Game 3, to 2-1 lead — Victor Wembanyama delivered another masterpiece with 39 points, 15 rebounds and more game-wrecking defence for the San Antonio Spurs, who took a 2-1 lead in the second-round NBA playoff series with a 115-108 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves on Friday night.

items.blurb: Mike Schmitz Hired As General Manager Of Mavericks — The Dallas Mavericks have named Mike Schmitz as General Manager, the team announced Friday. Schmitz will report to President Masai Ujiri and oversee day-to-day basketball operations and strategic planning. items.blurb: Brandon Ingram Undergoes Heel Surgery, Expected Ready For Training Camp — Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram underwent surgery Friday to remove a heel spur from his right foot, the team announced. items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama: Erupts for 39 points with 15 boards — Wembanyama chipped in 39 points (13-18 FG, 3-5 3Pt, 10-12 FT), 15 rebounds, one assist, five blocks and one steal across 37 minutes during Friday's 115-108 victory over Minnesota in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals. items.blurb: Anthony Edwards: Double-double in Game 3 loss — Edwards finished Friday's 115-108 loss to San Antonio in Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals with 32 points (12-26 FG, 3-9 3Pt, 5-6 FT), 14 rebounds, six assists and one block in 41 minutes. items.blurb: PJ Tucker Announces Retirement From NBA — PJ Tucker announced his retirement from the NBA in an Instagram post on Thursday. "20 years being my job but 40 plus years of not being able to fathom doing anything other than it. So here's to retiring from the NBA... because I will NEVER stop ballin," wrote Tucker. items.blurb: Thunder literally steal Game 2 from Lakers with second-half surge — The Thunder finished with as many steals as turnovers (12) as the Lakers coughed the ball up 21 times in the game.
